newsSince: No material new company-specific event since June 9: Q2 FY2026 (record revenue $384.4M, EPS $0.40 beat, record adj. EBITDA $97.2M, guidance raise) was reported May 5-6, before the window. The only fresh item is a June 21 sell-side note framing rising data-center/AI water-infrastructure demand and Sentryx/EchoShore digital growth, with a ~$32 fair-value gap; commentary, not a fundamental change.

sources.label: MWA undervalued on AI water demand (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Thermal & water latent-node thesis (valves, hydrants, leak-detection/monitoring as AI campuses stress municipal water) gets soft reinforcement from June 21 AI-water-demand framing, but the only hard data (Q2 beat) predates June 9. +1 sentiment credit, no move.
verdicts.bull: AI/data-center water stress elevates strategic value of pipes, valves and Sentryx monitoring.
verdicts.neutral: Strong Q2 already known; AI linkage still narrative-driven.
verdicts.bear: Municipal-capex cyclicality; AI-water tie is indirect.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict screen (water distribution, leak detection, smart-utility as AI campuses pressure municipal networks) unchanged; June 21 commentary mildly supports the AI-water linkage but adds no hard catalyst. A top-5 US#4 slot stays; +1 for steady narrative confirmation.
verdicts.bull: Smart-utility/leak-detection products gain relevance as AI campuses strain water grids.
verdicts.neutral: Top-5 already reflects the thesis; no fresh fundamental driver.
verdicts.bear: AI-campus water linkage remains speculative versus core municipal demand.
